en: Automobile/Motor Vehicle Liability

“Commercial Vehicles” are vehicles that are registered, insured, and maintained by the company, used exclusively for business purposes and driven by employees during work hours.

If your company selects ‘no,’ you may still need to provide proof of Automobile Liability Insurance, such as coverage for Non-Owned and/or Hired Automobile.
Does your company own or operate any commercial vehicles?
No
Yes

Provide professional services (e.g., architectural design, engineering, consulting, surveying, etc.) and/or are required to maintain a Professional Liability policy

Provide rigging operations, or any work involving the use of a crane, hook, or pulley system to move, build, or install machinery, and/or are required to maintain a Rigger’s Liability policy
Work near hazardous chemicals, contaminants, or pollutants and/or are required to maintain a Sudden and Accidental Pollution Endorsement on your General Liability policy
Work directly with or bring onto site hazardous chemicals, contaminants, or pollutants and/or are required to maintain a Pollution Liability policy

Transport hazardous chemicals, contaminants, or pollutants by vehicle, and/or are required to maintain an MCS-90 and/or Broadened Transportation Liability Endorsement (CA 9948) on an Automobile Liability policy
Transport by vehicle, cargo owned by or being shipped to a client facility and/or are required to maintain a Cargo Insurance policy
Bring onto site property (equipment, machinery, etc.) valued at $100,000 or more and/or are required to maintain a Contractor’s Equipment Insurance policy
Bring onto site any tools or materials used in the process of installation and/or are required to maintain an Installation Floater Insurance policy
Lead or oversee new commercial construction or major renovation projects and/or are required to maintain a Builder’s Risk Insurance policy
Have access to customers’ personal property, such as cash, securities, or other assets, and/or are required to maintain a Commercial Crime Liability policy

Does your company complete any work activities for current, future, or potential client projects involving marine, aircraft and/or railroads?\n\nYes, my company completes the following work activities (check all that apply):
Operate marine vessels and/or are required to maintain a Marine Protection & Indemnity (P&I) Insurance policy

Operate unmanned aircraft, including drones and/or are required to maintain an Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV)/Drone Liability policy

Perform work within 50 feet of a railroad right-of-way or affecting railroad property, including but not limited to tracks, bridges, tunnels, and switches, and/or are required to maintain a Railroad Protective Liability policy

Do you perform any of the following work relative to assignment of a risk level?

Activities considered “high risk” including work with chemicals/hazardous materials that require Safety Data Sheets (SDS), work from heights or confined spaces, hot work, tower climbing, major construction, demolition/explosives handling, or operating heavy mobile equipment such as lifts, cranes, excavators, etc.

Activities considered “medium risk” including driving a forklift, equipment testing, valves, road maintenance/repair, welding, transportation other than hazardous materials and precious cargo, food handling, HVAC repair/installation
No, none of the above
